title: 10 best herbal essential oils for uterine fibroids
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for uterine fibroids are natural extracts that help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids, non-cancerous growths in or around the uterus that can cause heavy bleeding, pain, and discomfort. The best herbal essential oils for uterine fibroids include Vitex agnus-castus, Angelica archangelica, Paeonia lactiflora, Urtica dioica, Ginkgo biloba, Trifolium pratense, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ulmus rubra, Lavandula angustifolia, and Cimicifuga racemosa. If left untreated, uterine fibroids can cause fatigue, anxiety, and social isolation, impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica essential oils helps with uterine fibroids because of its natural ability to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce inflammation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to ease symptoms such as heavy bleeding, cramping, and bloating, while its estrogen-balancing effects can slow down the growth of fibroid tumors. Additionally, angelica essential oils have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce scar tissue buildup in the uterus, allowing for a more healthy and balanced uterine environment. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Angelica essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant: Angelica essential oil is rich in antioxidants, which can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to the growth and development of uterine fibroids, promoting healthy tissue and cell growth.

Uterine tonic: Angelica essential oil has been traditionally used as a uterine tonic, helping to regulate menstrual cycles, reduce heavy bleeding, and ease cramps associated with uterine fibroids by toning and balancing the uterine muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with uterine fibroids.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of volatile oils that help to relax the uterus and alleviate menstrual cramps, making them beneficial for relieving symptoms of uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in the morning after dew has dried off the plants.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by gently rinsing with distilled water and patting dry with paper towels.
Steam-distill the plant material using a still or a steam distillation apparatus at 100-150°f for 1 hour.
Collect the resulting essential oil by separating it from the water using a separatory funnel and store in dark glass bottles.
Combine the freshly distilled angelica archangelica essential oil with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a 3:7 ratio and store in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree essential oils helps with uterine fibroids because of its natural ability to balance hormones. The oil contains phytoestrogens, which mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to the growth of uterine fibroids. Additionally, chaste tree essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pelvic pain, and cramping associated with uterine fibroids. By promoting hormone balance and reducing inflammation, chaste tree essential oils may provide natural relief from these uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory property can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, alleviating discomfort and pain.
Hormone-regulating: Chasteberry essential oil is known to balance hormone levels, which can help regulate the growth of uterine fibroids caused by hormonal imbalances.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Herbal Chaste Tree Essential Oil may help protect against oxidative stress and damage to cells, reducing the risk of fibroid tissue growth and potentially slowing down tumor progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, roots are known to have a relaxing effect on the uterine muscles, making them effective in reducing symptoms of uterine fibroids.
Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly azulene, which is believed to help reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitex agnus castus flowers in the morning when they are still dewy, and dry them thoroughly for 7 days.
Combine 500 grams of dried vitex agnus castus flowers with 2000 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steam distill the mixture using a double boiler setup with a condenser coil over low heat for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distillation process using cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before using it, allowing any sediment to settle to the bottom of the bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from organic farms.
Dry the harvested herbs in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days using paper bags.
Combine 50 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with 500 ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure uniform extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and discard the solids, allowing the oil to mature for an additional 7 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems at dawn when the dew is still present.
Wash the harvested plant material with distilled water to remove impurities and contaminants thoroughly.
Steam distill the washed plant material using a steam distillation apparatus with a 10:1 ratio of water to plant material for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a clean glass vial, filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and debris completely.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots in early morning after dew has evaporated.
Wash the harvested roots with filtered water and mild soap.
Steam distill the roots for 30 minutes with a ratio of 1 part roots to 4 parts water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and transfer it to dark glass vials within 24 hours.
Store the prepared essential oil in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with uterine fibroids because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve circulation. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate discomfort and cramping associated with uterine fibroids, while its muscle-relaxing effects can ease painful menstruation and bowel movements. Additionally, valerian oil's improved circulation helps to reduce swelling and promote the removal of toxins from the body, which may contribute to the reduction in fibroid size and alleviation of symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and dry them properly in a cool dark place for at least four weeks.
Steam distill the dried valeriana officinalis roots using a clevenger apparatus or a similar setup at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 4-6 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from any impurities using a centrifuge or filtering through a coffee filter paper.
Store the essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, protected from direct sunlight and moisture, at room temperature for up to six months.
Use 10-15 drops of the valeriana officinalis essential oil diluted in a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil and apply topically three times daily for best results.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with uterine fibroids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Transfer dried echinacea flowers to a clean glass jar and store in an airtight container.
Steep 1 cup of dried echinacea flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 8 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids then store the essential oil in dark glass bottles.

Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with uterine fibroids because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The oil contains compounds like fernandulcin, which can help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with fibroid growth. remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extract has antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which could potentially alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall, collecting at least 100 grams of fresh material.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Grind 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a clean glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 14-16 hours.

Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the herbal essential oil in an amber glass bottle at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest c monogyna leaves at dawn on sunny days when dew is still present in may and june for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested c monogyna leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours or air-drying for 7-10 days.
Steam distill 200g of dried c monogyna leaves with 1 liter of water in a stainless steel apparatus for 4 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the distilled essential oil using a coffee filter and collect the resulting crataegus monogyna essential oil in a dark glass bottle.
Store the crataegus monogyna essential oil at room temperature in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months to preserve its potency.
